organizations.name: Folds of Honor
organizations.eligibility: Children or spouses of U.S. Armed Service members who died, have a VA disability rating, are disabled due to service, or are on active duty with a Purple Heart.
organizations.coverage: Tuition, fees, on-campus housing, meal plans, required textbooks. Based on unmet financial need after other aid. Does NOT count loans or work-study as aid.
organizations.requirements: 2.0+ GPA for two most recent terms. Funds go directly to the school.
organizations.notes: Strong option for 100% P&T families. Need-based, so if Conroy/Ch.35 covers tuition, remaining housing/meals/books become the 'unmet need.' Apply every year.

organizations.name: American Legion Legacy Scholarship
organizations.eligibility: Children of post-9/11 veterans with 50%+ VA disability rating or who died on active duty.
organizations.coverage: Tuition, books, room and board, meal plans, supplies. Needs-based.
organizations.requirements: Parent must be a member in good standing of The American Legion. No multi-year membership requirement for Legacy (unlike Auxiliary scholarships). Recipients may reapply up to six times.
organizations.notes: Join the Legion ($50/yr) to qualify your kids. No waiting period for this specific scholarship. Stacks with federal and state benefits.

organizations.name: American Legion Auxiliary Scholarships
organizations.eligibility: Dependents must be ALA members. Various programs have different requirements.
organizations.coverage: Tuition, fees, books, room and board depending on program.
organizations.requirements: Spirit of Youth: 3 consecutive years of Junior ALA membership. Non-Traditional Student: 2 preceding years + current year membership. Plan ahead.
organizations.notes: Long game — enroll kids in ALA Junior membership NOW. By the time younger children hit college, they'll have the tenure requirement met. ~$25-35/yr per child.

organizations.name: DAV (Disabled American Veterans)
organizations.requirements: DAV membership is FREE for disabled veterans. Join DAV, then dependents can access Auxiliary scholarships.
organizations.notes: Free membership makes this a no-brainer. Even if the scholarship amount is smaller, it stacks with everything else.

organizations.name: VFW (Veterans of Foreign Wars)
organizations.eligibility: Dependents of VFW members. Various essay contests and scholarship programs.
organizations.requirements: VFW membership (~$45/yr). Must have served in a foreign conflict — OEF/OIF qualifies.
organizations.notes: Multiple scholarship tracks including Voice of Democracy essay contest ($30K top prize) and Patriot's Pen. Worth joining if you qualify.

organizations.name: No Greater Sacrifice
organizations.eligibility: Children (under 26) of post-9/11 veterans with 60%+ VA disability rating or who were killed/wounded in action.
organizations.coverage: Tuition, room, board, books — covers the full gap after other financial aid.
organizations.requirements: Competitive. Once selected, no annual reapplication needed.
organizations.notes: Potentially the most impactful single scholarship. If awarded, it covers everything other sources don't. Worth applying even if chances are competitive.

organizations.name: ThanksUSA
organizations.eligibility: Children (under 24) and spouses of active duty, reserve, guard, or retired military.
organizations.requirements: Easy application. Straightforward eligibility for military families.
organizations.notes: Low barrier to entry. Retired military dependents qualify — not just active duty.

organizations.name: Fisher House — Scholarships for Military Children
organizations.eligibility: Unmarried dependent children (under 23) of active duty, retired, or reserve military with valid military dependent ID.
organizations.coverage: Education expenses. Applied through commissary locations.
organizations.deadline: Early February (typically closes first week). Check site for exact dates.
organizations.requirements: Must have military dependent ID (DEERS enrollment). At least one scholarship awarded per commissary location. Maintain 3.0+ GPA.
organizations.notes: 100% P&T retirees have commissary access and DEERS-enrolled dependents. Easy $2K per kid if you don't miss the deadline — it closes early.

organizations.name: Freedom Alliance Scholarship Fund
organizations.eligibility: Children (under 26) of service members who are 100% permanently and totally disabled as a DIRECT RESULT of a combat mission. Priority to those with a single combat-related condition rated 70%+.
organizations.coverage: Tuition and expenses based on unmet need.
organizations.deadline: April (annual, check site for exact date)
organizations.requirements: Disability must stem from combat mission specifically — not just service-connected. Submit VA decision letter showing rated conditions.
organizations.notes: The combat-related requirement is strict. If your P&T rating is from non-combat service conditions, you likely won't qualify. Worth submitting and letting them make the determination.

organizations.name: Patriot Legacy Fund
organizations.eligibility: Children of veterans with 10%+ service-connected disability.
organizations.notes: Low disability threshold (10%+). Community service involvement strengthens the application.

organizations.name: Fight4Vets Dependent Scholarship
organizations.notes: Broad eligibility. Later deadline gives you time after the spring rush.

organizations.name: MOAA Scholarship Fund
organizations.eligibility: Students with veteran or active-duty military parents from all ranks and branches.
organizations.requirements: Check MOAA website for specific program requirements.
organizations.notes: Multiple award types — scholarships (free money), grants, and interest-free loans. Worth exploring all tracks.

organizations.name: Wounded Warrior Project — Warriors to Work
organizations.eligibility: Post-9/11 veterans who incurred a service-connected wound, illness, or injury.
organizations.coverage: Career counseling, resume assistance, job placement, networking. Some education support available.
organizations.notes: More career-focused than scholarship-focused, but valuable transition support. Free to eligible veterans.

organizations.name: Semper Fi & America's Fund
organizations.eligibility: Combat wounded, critically ill, or catastrophically injured service members and veterans (all branches despite the name).
organizations.coverage: Education assistance, family support, adaptive housing, transportation.
organizations.requirements: Must have been wounded/injured in combat or training operations.
organizations.notes: Despite the Marine-sounding name, this serves all branches. Provides direct financial assistance for education among other needs.
